summary: Increased neutrophil migratory activity is implicated as a contributing mechanism in traumatic lung injury due to its association with systemic inflammation and subsequent alveolar damage characteristic of ARDS. This supports the hypothesis that such immune responses are part of the pathophysiological cascade linking major trauma to acute respiratory distress syndrome.
